text.title: Decision Analysis Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the decision-analysis methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale and is referenced from `content/06-decision-tree.xml`.

rule.statement: Step 1 (define decision): Write outcome-shaped statement; include status quo in option set.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the define decision gate.

rule.statement: Step 2 (generate options): Divergence pass; minimum 3 options including status quo.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the generate options gate.

rule.statement: Step 3 (elicit weights): Individual weight elicitation; emit per-stakeholder vector.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the elicit weights gate.

rule.statement: Step 4 (reconcile and score): Reconcile to group weights; score each option × criterion by SME.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the reconcile and score gate.

rule.statement: Step 5 (sensitivity and document): One-at-a-time perturbation; document recommendation + dissent + traceability.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the sensitivity and document gate.

rule.statement: Step 6 (review and sign off): Governance review; capture dissent; tag and archive.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the review and sign off gate.

rule.statement: If preconditions for decision-analysis do not hold, do not apply this methodology; route to an alternative as the decision tree suggests.
rule.rationale: Methodologies misapplied to wrong contexts create overhead without value; explicit skip preserves the agent's budget.
